content:
The user wants me to extract the content of the provided image and format it as JSON. **1. Analyze the image:** - The image is a page from a technical datasheet, specifically for the STUSB4500. - It contains text, tables, and headings. - The main sections are: - A bullet point describing the behavior of POWER_OK pins. - A subsection titled "2.2.9 GPIO". - A description of the GPIO pin. - A table (Table 5) describing GPIO pin configuration. - A subsection titled "2.2.10 VBUS_EN_SNK". - A description of the VBUS_EN_SNK pin. - A table (Table 6) describing VBUS_EN_SNK pin configuration. - Text below Table 6 explaining the behavior of the pin. **2. Extract content section by section:** * **Header:** * Logo: ST * Part Number: STUSB4500 * Description: Pin description * **Bullet point:** * "At detachment the POWER_OK pins remain enabled (if already asserted), whereas VBUS_EN_SNK is disabled (Hi-Z) to deactivate the VBUS power path from the USB Type-C receptacle. The POWER_OK pins state is reinitialized (Hi-Z) after new attachment or after a reset" * **Section 2.2.9 GPIO:** * Title: 2.2.9 GPIO * Text: "This pin is an active low open drain output that can be configured by NVM as per table below (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)." * **Table 5:** * Title: Table 5. GPIO pin configuration * Columns: NVM parameter GPIO_CFG[1:0], Pin name, Pin function, Value, Description * Rows: * 00b, SW_CTRL_GPIO, Software controlled GPIO. The output state is defined by the value of I²C register bit #0 at address 2Dh, Hi-Z, When bit #0 value is 0b (at start-up) * (merged cell for 00b), (merged cell for SW_CTRL_GPIO), (merged cell for description), 0, When bit #0 value is 1b * 01b (default), ERROR_RECOVERY, Hardware fault detection (see Section 3.7 Hardware fault management), Hi-Z, No hardware fault detected * (merged cell for 01b), (merged cell for ERROR_RECOVERY), (merged cell for description), 0, Hardware fault detected * 10b, DEBUG, Debug accessory detection (see Section 3.8 Debug accessory mode detection), Hi-Z, No debug accessory detected * (merged cell for 10b), (merged cell for DEBUG), (merged cell for description), 0, Debug accessory detected * 11b, SINK_POWER, Indicates USB Type-C current capability advertised by the source, Hi-Z, Source supplies default or 1.5 A USB Type-C current at 5 V * (merged cell for 11b), (merged cell for SINK_POWER), (merged cell for description), 0, Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C current at 5 V * **Section 2.2.10 VBUS_EN_SNK:** * Title: 2.2.10 VBUS_EN_SNK * Text: "This pin allows the incoming VBUS power from the USB Type-C receptacle to be enabled when a source is connected according to different operating conditions stated in the table below. The default behavior of the pin can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)." * **Table 6:** * Title: Table 6. VBUS_EN_SNK pin configuration * Columns: NVM parameter POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5V, Pin function, Value, Description, Comment * Rows: * 0b (default), Enables VBUS power path when source attached whatever VBUS voltage (5 V or any PDO voltage), Hi-Z, No source attached, Valid for all POWER_OK pin configurations 1, 2 and 3 * (merged cell for 0b), (merged cell for description), 0, Source attached, (merged cell for comment) * 1b, Enables VBUS power path only when source attached and VBUS voltage negotiated to PDO2 or PDO3 voltage, Hi-Z, No source attached or no PD explicit contract with PDO2 or PDO3, (merged cell for comment) * (merged cell for 1b), (merged cell for description), 0, Source attached and PD explicit contract with PDO2 or PDO3, (merged cell for comment) * **Text below Table 6:** * "When POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5V bit is set to logic level high, the VBUS_EN_SNK pin is asserted only when a PDO2 or PDO3 explicit contract is established with the source (see Section 3.3 Auto-run mode)." * "This feature is suited for sink devices requiring high power charging profile above 5 V to be fully operational (see Section 6.1.2 Powering a system under high charging profile only)." * "VBUS_EN_SNK pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable the VBUS power path." * **Footer:** * DS12499 - Rev 8 * page 8/42 **3. Format as JSON:** Construct the JSON structure based on the extracted content. Ensure proper handling of merged cells in tables. ```json [{"bbox": [68, 66, 126, 91], "category": "figure", "text": ""}, {"bbox": [813, 66, 930, 96], "category": "header", "text": "STUSB4500\nPin description"}, {"bbox": [162, 120, 926, 162], "category": "text", "text": "- At detachment the POWER_OK pins remain enabled (if already asserted), whereas VBUS_EN_SNK is disabled (Hi-Z) to deactivate the $V_{BUS}$ power path from the USB Type-C receptacle. The POWER_OK pins state is reinitialized (Hi-Z) after new attachment or after a reset"}, {"bbox": [67, 177, 212, 191], "category": "title", "text": "2.2.9 GPIO"}, {"bbox": [161, 194, 822, 221], "category": "text", "text": "This pin is an active low open drain output that can be configured by NVM as per table below (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."}, {"bbox": [431, 240, 661, 254], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Table 5. GPIO pin configuration"}, {"bbox": [164, 259, 927, 507], "category": "table", "text": "
NVM parameter GPIO_CFG[1:0]Pin namePin functionValueDescription
00bSW_CTRL_GPIOSoftware controlled GPIO. The output state is defined by the value of I²C register bit #0 at address 2DhHi-ZWhen bit #0 value is 0b (at start-up)
0When bit #0 value is 1b
01b (default)ERROR_RECOVERYHardware fault detection (see Section 3.7 Hardware fault management)Hi-ZNo hardware fault detected
0Hardware fault detected
10bDEBUGDebug accessory detection (see Section 3.8 Debug accessory mode detection)Hi-ZNo debug accessory detected
0Debug accessory detected
11bSINK_POWERIndicates USB Type-C current capability advertised by the sourceHi-ZSource supplies default or 1.5 A USB Type-C current at 5 V
0Source supplies 3.0 A USB Type-C current at 5 V
"}, {"bbox": [67, 525, 294, 540], "category": "title", "text": "2.2.10 VBUS_EN_SNK"}, {"bbox": [161, 543, 902, 585], "category": "text", "text": "This pin allows the incoming $V_{BUS}$ power from the USB Type-C receptacle to be enabled when a source is connected according to different operating conditions stated in the table below. The default behavior of the pin can be changed by NVM programming (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."}, {"bbox": [395, 603, 698, 617], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Table 6. VBUS_EN_SNK pin configuration"}, {"bbox": [164, 622, 927, 794], "category": "table", "text": "
NVM parameter POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5VPin functionValueDescriptionComment
0b (default)Enables VBUS power path when source attached whatever VBUS voltage (5 V or any PDO voltage)Hi-ZNo source attachedValid for all POWER_OK pin configurations 1, 2 and 3
0Source attached
1bEnables VBUS power path only when source attached and VBUS voltage negotiated to PDO2 or PDO3 voltageHi-ZNo source attached or no PD explicit contract with PDO2 or PDO3
0Source attached and PD explicit contract with PDO2 or PDO3
"}, {"bbox": [161, 811, 915, 838], "category": "text", "text": "When POWER_ONLY_ABOVE_5V bit is set to logic level high, the VBUS_EN_SNK pin is asserted only when a PDO2 or PDO3 explicit contract is established with the source (see Section 3.3 Auto-run mode)."}, {"bbox": [161, 841, 918, 868], "category": "text", "text": "This feature is suited for sink devices requiring high power charging profile above 5 V to be fully operational (see Section 6.1.2 Powering a system under high charging profile only)."}, {"bbox": [161, 869, 894, 897], "category": "text", "text": "VBUS_EN_SNK pin is a high voltage open drain output that allows a PMOS transistor to be directly driven to enable the $V_{BUS}$ power path."}, {"bbox": [68, 973, 164, 984], "category": "footer", "text": "DS12499 - Rev 8"}, {"bbox": [872, 974, 930, 986], "category": "footer", "text": "page 8/42"}] ```
